reasoning: Butterfly is a non-rigid entity (it can cease to exist without ceasing to be the same butterfly) but it does carry identity conditions (if something is a butterfly, it cannot also be an apple or a car). It is unifying because an entity cannot cease to be a butterfly without ceasing to exist. It is independent as it represents a distinct physical entity.

Red is a rigid property because if something is red, it cannot cease to be red without ceasing to exist. It does not carry identity condition (−I) because it cannot distinguish individual instances of red objects by a condition that it alone satisfies. It does not supply its own IC (−O) nor is it anti-unifying (~U) because a collection or sum of red objects is a single red object. It is non-dependent (−D) as it represents a property that does not require the presence of a specific physical entity to exist.

Food is a general term (Sortal) that is non-rigid because an object can cease to be food without ceasing to exist. It carries an identity condition (+I) inherited from its instances and does not supply its own IC (-O). It is unifying (+U) because a single object can be an instance of food and it is independent (-D) as it represents a standalone concept.

The term 'Animal' is a non-rigid property because an instance (an animal) can cease to be an animal without ceasing to exist. It carries an identity condition (+I) inherited from its more specific instances (e.g., dogs, cats) that do not supply their own IC (-O). It is a unifying concept (+U) that applies to a collection of diverse entities (various species of animals) and is independent (-D) as it is not a sub-concept of a more general one.

RedApple is a rigid property because if something is a red apple, it cannot cease to be a red apple without ceasing to exist. It does not carry identity condition (it is a mereological sum of its parts) but does not supply its own IC. It is non-unifying as it cannot be a common property to other distinct entities, and it is independent because it does not require the presence of another entity to exist.

A Group is a non-rigid entity (it does not necessarily exist at all possible worlds) that does not carry identity conditions, as the members can change without losing its identity (it is an aggregate or sum of its constituents). It is unifying because it is an intrinsic whole-part relationship, and it is independent as it represents the existence of an intrinsic entity.

A country is a rigid property because if something is a country, it cannot cease to be a country without ceasing to exist. It carries an identity condition (it is possible to distinguish individual countries based on a set of necessary and sufficient features). It does not supply its own IC, as the criteria for identifying a country are more complex and depend on a combination of factors (e.g., a legal community, a specific territory, a government, and a population). It is non-unifying, as a single physical entity (e.g., a region) can be a part of more than one country. It is independent, as the existence of a country does not require the presence of a specific physical entity or a specific type of social organization.

AmountOfMatter is a non-rigid property (it can cease to exist without ceasing to be the same amount) that carries identity conditions (it is possible to distinguish between 'this' amount and 'that' amount). It is unifying because we can speak of a single amount of matter without necessarily implying the existence of a specific physical entity. It is independent as it represents a high-level property that does not require the presence of a specific lower-level entity to exist.

PhysicalObject is typically a rigid property because an instance cannot cease to be a physical object without ceasing to exist. It is said to carry identity (plus I) inherited from its backbone sortal. It does not supply its own identity condition (minus O) nor is it anti-unifying (~U) because a physical object is an intrinsic whole.

A GroupOfPeople is a non-rigid (~R) entity because a group can cease to exist while remaining the same type of entity (e.g., a group of friends can disband). It does not carry identity conditions (+I) inherited from its constituents, as the identity of a group is not solely dependent on the identity of its constituents. It does not supply its own IC (-O). It is unifying (+U) because it is an aggregate or sum of its constituents. It is independent (-D) as it represents a collection or sum of individuals rather than a specific physical entity.

SocialEntity is a high-level generalization that does not provide a specific rigid property. It does not carry an identity condition (it is an aggregate or sum of individuals rather than a single object), does not supply its own IC, is non-unifying (it is a generalization that includes wildly different individuals), and is independent (it is not a dependent-on-a-certain-criteria concept).

A location is non-rigid (it can cease to exist without ceasing to be a location) and does not carry or supply an identity condition. It is unifying because a single spatial or conceptual coordinate system can define a location. It is independent as it represents a space or position that does not require the presence of a specific physical entity to exist.

Fruit is a rigid property because an instance cannot cease to be a fruit without ceasing to exist. It does not supply its own identity condition (O-V) because it is a high-level generalization that does not define a specific set of necessary and sufficient conditions for its instances. It is non-unifying (~U) because a single entity (e.g., an apple or a peach) can be a fruit and still be a more specific kind (e.g., a type of fruit or a type of edible). It is independent (~D) as it represents a distinct property that does not require the presence of another entity to exist.

Vertebrate is a rigid property because an instance cannot cease to be a vertebrate without ceasing to exist. It carries identity conditions (I) inherited from the higher-level concept 'Animal'. It does not supply its own IC conditions (O), as it is a more specific concept than the most basic IC-bearing entities (e.g., living being). It is unifying (+U) because the diverse entities (e.g., mammals, reptiles) that are vertebrates share a common unity criterion. It is independent (-D) as it represents a concept that does not require the presence of another to exist.

A Caterpillar is a non-rigid entity (it can cease to exist while remaining the same caterpillar) that does not carry identity conditions, as it is a common-sense term. It does not supply its own IC, but is a unifying concept (an instance cannot cease to be a caterpillar without losing its nature). It is independent as it represents a specific stage in an organism's life cycle.

A Person is a rigid property because if something is a person, it cannot cease to be a person without ceasing to exist. It carries an identity condition (plus I) inherited from its backbone sortal, but does not unify a lower-level entity (e.g., an action or a part of the body) within itself (minor anti-unity, ~U). It is independent but does not supply its own IC (it inherits it from a more general sortal, usually 'Natural Kind' or 'Rigid +I' in the ontology).

A LegalAgent is a generalized concept (Sortal) that does not provide its own identity condition, but it is non-rigid (it can cease to exist without ceasing to be a legal agent) and it is externally unified (it is a single entity or person or corporation that is recognized as a legal agent). It is independent as it represents a distinct concept.

An organization is a non-rigid entity (it can cease to exist or change its nature) that carries identity (it is possible to distinguish individual organizations from one another). It does not supply its own identity condition, as it is a high-level concept that depends on the existence of agents or a social community. It is a unifying concept because it is used to group together agents or social communities to form a single entity.

Agent is a rigid property because if something is an agent, it cannot cease to be an agent without ceasing to exist. It carries identity conditions (spatio-temporal continuity) and is a unifying concept (it applies to an intrinsic whole). It is non-dependent because the existence of an agent does not require the existence of another entity.

An apple is a non-rigid entity (because an instance of an apple can change over time or lose parts), but it does not supply its own identity condition (it inherits the 'fruit' and 'appletree' conditions). It is a unifying concept (it is one organic whole) and independent as it does not require the presence of another entity to exist.
